name: Las Cuatro Calas — Running from Águilas
name_original: Las Cuatro Calas — Running desde Águilas
region_intro: Jogging routes around Águilas offer a diverse range of terrains, from extensive coastlines and sandy beaches to more challenging mountainous and hilly areas. The region features 34 kilometers of coastline with numerous beaches and coves, providing varied running environments. Beyond the coastal paths, runners can explore trails through Mediterranean landscapes in areas like Sierra de Almenara and protected natural spaces. These varied landscapes ensure options for different preferences and fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Águilas The most popular jogging route is Las Cuatro Calas — Running from Águilas, a 5.8 miles (9.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 7 minutes to complete. This route offers views of multiple coves within a protected landscape. Another top favourite among local runners is Running circular at Cocón de Cope, a moderate 3.9 miles (6.3 km) path. This circular route provides elevation gain and views of the coast of the Guadalentín Valley. Local runners also love the Cala Mijo – Coastal Trail loop from Paisaje Protegido Cuatro Calas, a 5.1 miles (8.1 km) trail leading through coastal scenery, often completed in about 55 minutes. Jogging around Águilas is defined by extensive coastlines, protected natural areas, and hilly terrains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy beach runs to more challenging trails with elevation. The routes in Águilas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 1000 runners have used komoot to explore Águilas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Águilas?
A: Águilas offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 60 trails available for exploration. These routes cater to various preferences, from coastal paths to more challenging hilly terrains.

Q: Are there easy jogging routes suitable for beginners in Águilas?
A: Yes, Águilas has several easy running routes perfect for beginners. There are 8 routes classified as easy, often found along the town's extensive promenades and urban beaches like Playa de Las Delicias, offering mostly paved surfaces and gentle inclines.

Q: Where can I find running routes with scenic sea views in Águilas?
A: Many running routes in Águilas boast spectacular sea views due to its extensive coastline. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e900997494/las-cuatro-calas-running-from-aguilas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Cuatro Calas — Running from Águilas&lt;/a&gt; trail, which offers views of multiple coves, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33931717" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cala Mijo – Coastal Trail loop&lt;/a&gt;, leading through beautiful coastal scenery.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Águilas?
A: Yes, Águilas features several circular running routes. A notable option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e900801672/running-circular-at-cocon-de-cope" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running circular at Cocón de Cope&lt;/a&gt;, which provides elevation gain and panoramic views of the Guadalentín Valley's coast. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31546436" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de la Colonia loop&lt;/a&gt; is another moderate circular route, much of which is paved.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect on the running trails around Águilas?
A: The running trails around Águilas offer a diverse range of natural landscapes. You can enjoy extensive coastlines and sandy beaches, coastal trails hugging cliffs with Mediterranean views, and more challenging mountainous and hilly terrains in areas like the Sierra de Almenara. Protected natural areas also provide unspoiled scenery.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions to see along the running routes?
A: Absolutely! Águilas combines natural beauty with historical interest. You might encounter the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/535410" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cope Tower&lt;/a&gt;, the historic Hornillo Pier, or the characteristic black-and-white striped Lighthouse. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3141198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cocón de Cope&lt;/a&gt; area also offers stunning viewpoints.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Águilas?
A: The running routes in Águilas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars. Runners often praise the varied terrain, from beautiful coastal paths to trails with elevation, and the opportunity to explore both urban beaches and more solitary coves. The well-maintained routes and pleasant weather are also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any running routes that are particularly good for families?
A: For families, the town's long promenade along the bay and urban beaches like Playa de Las Delicias offer pleasant, mostly paved surfaces ideal for leisurely runs or walks. These areas provide easy access and a safe environment for all ages.

Q: Can I find more challenging running trails with significant elevation in Águilas?
A: Yes, for those seeking a challenge, Águilas offers trails with elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e900801672/running-circular-at-cocon-de-cope" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running circular at Cocón de Cope&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate path with notable elevation, providing expansive views. The Sierra de Almenara also features routes through Mediterranean landscapes that can be more demanding.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Águilas?
A: Águilas is known for its favorable year-round weather, making it an excellent destination for running in most seasons. Spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant temperatures, while even winter provides mild conditions. During summer, early mornings or late evenings are ideal to avoid the midday heat.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by unique natural monuments or beaches?
A: Many routes in Águilas will take you past stunning natural features. For example, the area around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4085357" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Cocedores Beach&lt;/a&gt; is part of a blue trail offering insights into local history and wildlife. You can also discover beautiful spots like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4201747" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Higuerica Beach&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7156224" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Percheles Beach&lt;/a&gt; on various coastal runs.
tips: Nice and quiet beach near the town of Calareona. You can get here by following the GR-253 path that runs along the coast and you will have a wonderful landscape throughout your journey.
